steps.title: Calculate distance traveled at constant speed
steps.content:
Dana first drove 24.0 miles, then drove at a constant speed of 68.0 miles per hour for 2 hours. Use the relationship distance = rate times time to find the distance traveled during the second segment:
$$\text{Distance} = 68.0 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 136 \text{ miles}$$

steps.title: Add both segments to find total distance
steps.content:
The total distance Dana traveled is the sum of the initial 24.0 miles and the 136 miles traveled at constant speed:
$$\text{Total Distance} = 24.0 + 136 = 160 \text{ miles}$$
